headline: Gluware Appoints Alex Henthorn-Iwane as Senior Vice President of Marketing
summary: Gluware, the leader in intelligent network automation, today announced the appointment of Alex Henthorn-Iwane as Senior Vice President of Marketing. Henthorn-Iwane is a veteran enterprise networking marketing executive with a track record of driving GTM growth at companies that went on to major acquisitions. Most notable among these are ThousandEyes, where he served as VP of Product Marketing ahead of its acquisition by Cisco, and Sinefa, where he led marketing ahead of its acquisition by Palo A

headline: Cisco Advances 5% to Record Highs on AI Cybersecurity Push, Arista Climbs as Networking Trade Extends
summary: Shares of Cisco Systems (NASDAQ:CSCO) are up 5% in midday trading on Tuesday, June 2, changing hands at $127 and change after a Monday close of $121.33. headline: NATO AI Security Role Puts Focus On Palo Alto Networks Earnings Story
summary: NATO has selected Palo Alto Networks (NasdaqGS:PANW) as a non commercial cybersecurity partner focused on government level AI security. The partnership centers on securing AI systems used across the NATO alliance, highlighting the role of AI focused defenses in public sector cybersecurity. The announcement arrives ahead of Palo Alto Networks’ upcoming earnings report, adding a fresh policy and security angle to the investment story. Palo Alto Networks operates in network, cloud, and...

headline: A Once-in-a-Generation Megatrend Makes This Cybersecurity Stock a High-Conviction Buy for the Next 20 Years
summary: Palo Alto Networks (NASDAQ:PANW) is positioned as a long-duration compounder because cybersecurity has crossed the line from corporate luxury into non-discretionary utility, and the company sits at the center of that permanent shift. Hackers don’t take time off when the economy slows down, and enterprises are now consolidating fragmented security stacks onto integrated, AI-driven platforms. ... headline: Stock Index Futures Muted as Investors Parse Middle East Developments, U.S. JOLTS Report in Focus
summary: June S&P 500 E-Mini futures (ESM26) are down -0.14%, and June Nasdaq 100 E-Mini futures (NQM26) are down -0.01% this morning, taking a breather after a recent rally, while investors assess the prospects for an end to the Middle East conflict.
